content: Investment income is taxed differently based on source and holding period. Long-term capital gains (assets held >1 year) are taxed at preferential rates (0%, 15%, 20%) compared to ordinary income. Short-term gains are taxed as ordinary income. Dividends are classified as 'qualified' (lower rate) or 'ordinary'. The Wash Sale Rule disallows claiming a loss if the same security is repurchased within 30 days.
